---
name: parallel-dev
type: skill
description: >
  Execution-grade standard for PARALLEL_DEV.md documentation. Enforces a canonical
  10-section structure: Overview, Model Routing, Mode Definitions, Mode Selection,
  Phase Grouping Rules, Token Optimization, Resilience Framework, Status Display,
  Requirements, and Fully Autonomous Pipeline. Language-agnostic: quality gate hooks
  are kept in scripts/hooks/ so run_parallel.sh stays language-independent.
  Compliant with run_parallel.sh, config/phases.yaml, and phase-builder.agent.md.
---

# Parallel Dev — Documentation Standard

Every `docs/PARALLEL_DEV.md` MUST contain exactly the ten sections below, in order.
Missing sections make the document incomplete for operator use. Missing compliance
points make it incompatible with `run_parallel.sh`.

This skill is **language-agnostic**. Language-specific commands (test runners, linters,
compile checks) belong in `scripts/hooks/quality-gates.sh` — never in `run_parallel.sh`
itself. Use the **Language Reference Table** in `roadmap-spec/SKILL.md` to fill in the
hook implementation examples.

---

## Relationship to the Pipeline Toolchain

| Component                           | How it uses PARALLEL_DEV.md                                                        |
| ----------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `run_parallel.sh`                   | Operator reference — modes, retry limits, model routing all defined here           |
| `config/phases.yaml`                | Phase `name`, `group`, `complexity`, `skills` feed directly into `run_parallel.sh` |
| `phase-builder.agent.md`            | Reads `PHASE_TASK.md` generated by `run_parallel.sh` from `phases.yaml` metadata   |
| `scripts/hooks/quality-gates.sh`    | Language-specific gates — called by `run_parallel.sh` with a non-zero = failure    |
| `AGENT_PIPELINE` (run_parallel.sh)  | `phase-builder → dto-guardian → integration → security-auditor → test-builder`     |
| `PROTECTED_PATHS` (run_parallel.sh) | `contracts/`, `database/`, `docs/` — additive-only enforced during merge           |

---

## Language Reference Table

Use this table to fill in quality gate hook examples and requirements sections.
Language-specific tools go in `scripts/hooks/quality-gates.sh` — NOT in `run_parallel.sh`.

| Concept             | Python                                        | Go                                     | TypeScript                             | Node.js                                | Rust                             | Java                               |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `{test-cmd}`        | `pytest tests/ -q`                            | `go test ./...`                        | `npm test`                             | `npm test`                             | `cargo test`                     | `mvn test`                         |
| `{lint-cmd}`        | `ruff check .`                                | `golangci-lint`                        | `eslint src/`                          | `eslint src/`                          | `cargo clippy`                   | `mvn checkstyle`                   |
| `{compile-check}`   | `python -m py_compile`                        | `go build ./...`                       | `tsc --noEmit`                         | `node --check`                         | `cargo check`                    | `mvn compile`                      |
| `{module-dir}`      | `app/modules/`                                | `internal/modules/`                    | `src/modules/`                         | `src/modules/`                         | `src/modules/`                   | `src/main/java/com/app/modules/`   |
| `{contracts}`       | `contracts/`                                  | `contracts/`                           | `contracts/`                           | `contracts/`                           | `src/contracts/`                 | `src/main/java/com/app/contracts/` |
| `{import-check}`    | `no direct DB driver imports in app/modules/` | `no database/sql in internal/modules/` | `no DB client imports in src/modules/` | `no DB driver imports in src/modules/` | `no sqlx/diesel in src/modules/` | `no JPA/JDBC in modules/`          |
| `{runtime-tool}`    | `python3`                                     | `go`                                   | `node` / `ts-node`                     | `node`                                 | `cargo`                          | `java` / `mvn`                     |
| `{package-manager}` | `pip` / `uv`                                  | `go mod`                               | `npm` / `pnpm`                         | `npm`                                  | `cargo`                          | `mvn` / `gradle`                   |

> **Rule:** Pick one language per project. Implement quality gates in `scripts/hooks/quality-gates.sh` using the appropriate commands from that language column.

---

## Canonical Section Order

```
1.  Overview
2.  Model Routing Strategy
3.  Mode Definitions
4.  Mode Selection Strategy
5.  Phase Grouping Rules
6.  Token Cost Optimization Strategy
7.  Resilience Framework
8.  Status Display
9.  Requirements
10. Fully Autonomous Pipeline
```

---

## Section Definitions

### 1. Overview

One to two paragraphs plus a tradeoffs table. State:

- What the script does and what it enables
- The three execution modes and what tradeoff each optimizes
- Where modules live and why isolation enables parallelism

**Required tradeoffs table:**

```markdown
| Dimension      | More Parallelism              | Less Parallelism        |
| -------------- | ----------------------------- | ----------------------- |
| **Speed**      | Faster wall-clock time        | Slower (sequential)     |
| **Token cost** | Higher (each agent re-reads)  | Lower (shared context)  |
| **Merge risk** | More conflicts at integration | Fewer conflicts         |
| **Debugging**  | Harder (concurrent sessions)  | Easier (single session) |
```

> **phases.yaml alignment:** State which directory houses the isolated modules
> (e.g., `app/modules/` for Python/TS/Node.js, `internal/modules/` for Go).
> This must match `{module-dir}` in `config/phases.yaml` phase scopes.

**Template:**

```markdown
## 1. Overview

This framework supports parallel development of pipeline phases. Most phases own isolated
modules under `{module-dir}` and communicate only through immutable DTOs in `{contracts}`.
This isolation enables parallel development — multiple phases implemented at the same time
by independent AI agents.

However, parallelism has tradeoffs:

| Dimension      | More Parallelism              | Less Parallelism        |
| -------------- | ----------------------------- | ----------------------- |
| **Speed**      | Faster wall-clock time        | Slower (sequential)     |
| **Token cost** | Higher (each agent re-reads)  | Lower (shared context)  |
| **Merge risk** | More conflicts at integration | Fewer conflicts         |
| **Debugging**  | Harder (concurrent sessions)  | Easier (single session) |

Three execution modes let the operator choose the right balance for the situation.
```

---

### 2. Model Routing Strategy

Define which model handles the heaviest phase/group and what rotation pool handles everything else. Must stay in sync with the `MODEL_HEAVY`, `MODEL_HEAVY_LITE`, and `MODEL_ROTATE_POOL` variables in `run_parallel.sh`.

**Required table:**

```markdown
| Mode                     | Heavy Model         | Used For                               |
| ------------------------ | ------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| Mode 1 (Full Parallel)   | `claude-opus-4.6`   | Heaviest phase (most complex by score) |
| Mode 2 (Token-Optimized) | `claude-sonnet-4.6` | Single session (all phases)            |
| Mode 3 (Hybrid)          | `claude-sonnet-4.6` | Heaviest group (by complexity score)   |
```

**Required rotation pool block:**

```markdown
**Rotation pool** (round-robin, used for all other phases and remediation agents):
```

claude-sonnet-4.6 → claude-sonnet-4.5 → gpt-5.3-codex → gpt-5.4

```

Used for: non-heavy phases, conflict-resolver, post-merge review, docs sync, quality gate
remediation, and integration remediation.
```

**Required env overrides block:**

````markdown
**Environment overrides:**

```bash
MODEL_HEAVY="claude-opus-4.6"           # Override Mode 1 heavy model
MODEL_HEAVY_LITE="claude-sonnet-4.6"    # Override Modes 2 & 3 heavy model
```
````

````

> **run_parallel.sh alignment:** Model names here MUST match the defaults in
> `MODEL_HEAVY`, `MODEL_HEAVY_LITE`, and `MODEL_ROTATE_POOL` array in the script.

---

### 3. Mode Definitions

One subsection per mode. Each subsection MUST contain:

1. One-line summary of the isolation model
2. ASCII branch diagram
3. Numbered step-by-step "how it works" list (mirroring what `run_parallel.sh` does)
4. "When to use" bullets

**Three modes — all must be documented:**

#### Mode 1 — Full Parallel (Maximum Speed)

```markdown
### Mode 1 — Full Parallel (Maximum Speed)

Each phase runs in a **separate Git worktree** with a **dedicated Copilot CLI agent**.
All phases execute simultaneously.

```text
main
 ├─ track/phase-N   ← worktree 1, checkpoint + agent pipeline (bounded retries)
 └─ track/phase-M   ← worktree 2, checkpoint + agent pipeline (bounded retries)
````

1. Creates a branch per phase from `main`
2. Creates a Git worktree per branch (sibling directories)
3. Generates a `PHASE_TASK.md` instruction file in each worktree
4. Creates checkpoint (`git tag checkpoint-phase-N-pre`) in each worktree
5. Runs `scripts/hooks/setup-env.sh` in each worktree (non-fatal if absent)
6. Runs the **agent pipeline** per worktree with **bounded retries**:
   - `scripts/hooks/activate-env.sh` — activates runtime env before agent
   - `phase-builder` — implements the phase (up to `MAX_RETRIES_PHASE_BUILDER` retries)
   - `dto-guardian` — validates DTO contracts (up to `MAX_RETRIES_DTO` retries)
   - `integration` — validates module wiring (up to `MAX_RETRIES_INTEGRATION` retries)
   - `security-auditor` — security review
   - `test-builder` — test generation
   - `refactor` — fixes quality gate failures (up to `MAX_REMEDIATION_RETRIES` retries)
   - If any stage exceeds retry limit → rollback to checkpoint
7. Tracks per-phase status in `.parallel-dev/phase-status.json`
8. Resource control: max `MAX_PARALLEL_AGENTS` (default 3) concurrent pipelines
9. Waits for all agent pipelines to finish
10. Auto-merges all branches into integration branch (union strategy, bounded retries)
11. Post-merge review via `merge-reviewer` agent
12. Documentation sync via `merge-reviewer` agent (advisory)
13. Global validation + orchestrator authority check
14. Creates PR automatically via `gh pr create`

**When to use:**

- Deadline pressure — need maximum throughput
- All phases in the batch are independent (no shared file ownership)

````

#### Mode 2 — Token-Optimized (Serial Grouping)

```markdown
### Mode 2 — Token-Optimized (Serial Grouping)

Multiple phases run **sequentially in a single Copilot CLI session**. No worktrees.

```text
main
 └─ track/group-N-M  ← single branch
     Phase N → commit → Phase M → commit
````

1. Creates a single branch from `main`
2. Generates a single `PHASE_TASK.md` with all phases in order
3. Creates checkpoint (`git tag checkpoint-group-X-pre`)
4. Runs agent pipeline sequentially — each phase committed before the next
5. Post-merge review, docs sync, global validation
6. Creates PR automatically

**Grouping rules:**

- Maximum 3 phases per session (context window limit)
- Phases must be in dependency order (earlier phases first)
- DTO-producing phases go before DTO-consuming phases

**When to use:**

- Cost-sensitive development (limited premium requests)
- Phases have sequential dependencies

````

#### Mode 3 — Hybrid (Balanced) — DEFAULT

```markdown
### Mode 3 — Hybrid (Balanced) — DEFAULT

Groups of phases run **in parallel across groups**, sequentially within each group.

```text
main
 ├─ track/group-a  ← worktree 1, all phases in group A run sequentially
 └─ track/group-b  ← worktree 2, all phases in group B run sequentially
````

Groups determined by `group` field in `config/phases.yaml`. Same group = sequential.
Different group = runs in parallel worktree.

**When to use:**

- Default choice for most development sessions
- Phases have natural groupings by pipeline section

````

> **phases.yaml alignment:** Groups in `config/phases.yaml` directly map to Mode 3
> worktrees. Document the group mapping here so operators can predict execution.

---

### 4. Mode Selection Strategy

Provide both a decision flowchart (ASCII) and a scenario table. Operators must be
able to pick the right mode in under 30 seconds.

**Required decision flowchart:**

```markdown
```text
                        ┌────────────────────┐
                        │   How many phases? │
                        └────────┬───────────┘
                                 │
                   ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
                   ▼             ▼              ▼
              1 phase      2–3 phases      4+ phases
                   │             │              │
                   ▼             ▼              ▼
             Mode 2         Mode 2          Independent?
          (single session)                  yes→Mode 1
                                            no→Mode 3
````

````

**Required scenario table:**

```markdown
| Scenario                                    | Recommended Mode |
| ------------------------------------------- | ---------------- |
| Single phase implementation                 | Mode 2           |
| 2–3 phases with sequential dependency       | Mode 2           |
| 2–3 fully independent phases                | Mode 1           |
| 4+ phases, mix of dependent and independent | Mode 3           |
| Cost-constrained (limited premium requests) | Mode 2           |
| Deadline pressure, all phases independent   | Mode 1           |
| Default / unsure                            | Mode 3           |
````

---

### 5. Phase Grouping Rules

Defines which phases can run in parallel and which cannot. Must map to the `group`
field in `config/phases.yaml`.

**Required subsections:**

1. **Safe Parallel Combinations** — phases that own disjoint files
2. **Unsafe Combinations** — phases that share protected paths or infrastructure
3. **File Ownership** — which phases own which directories

````markdown
### Safe Parallel Combinations

Phases can run simultaneously when they own **different files**:

```text
✅ Phase A ‖ Phase C  — different modules, no shared files
✅ Phase B ‖ Phase D  — independent inputs
```
````

### Unsafe Combinations

```text
❌ Phase 0 ‖ anything   — Phase 0 creates shared infrastructure
❌ DTO changes ‖ module changes — module depends on DTO definition
❌ orchestrator ‖ any module  — concurrent changes conflict
```

### File Ownership

| Phase   | Owns                                               | Group |
| ------- | -------------------------------------------------- | ----- |
| Phase 0 | `database/`, `{contracts}`, `{module-dir}/health/` | A     |
| Phase N | `{module-dir}/<name>/`, `{contracts}/<name>.*`     | B     |
| Phase M | `{module-dir}/<name>/`, `{contracts}/<name>.*`     | C     |

````

> Each row must match a phase entry in `config/phases.yaml`.
> Protected paths (`contracts/`, `database/`, `docs/`) are enforced in `run_parallel.sh`
> via `PROTECTED_PATHS`. Agents may add to them but never delete or modify existing content.

---

### 6. Token Cost Optimization Strategy

Documents the skill-first loading strategy that reduces agent token consumption.
Must reference the token savings table and list the five optimization rules.

**Required token savings table:**

```markdown
| Full Doc                    | Tokens | Equivalent Skill              | Tokens | Savings |
| --------------------------- | ------ | ----------------------------- | ------ | ------- |
| `docs/architecture.md`      | ~5000  | pipeline + modularity skills  | ~600   | 88%     |
| `docs/dto_contracts.md`     | ~6000  | dto skill                     | ~400   | 93%     |
| `docs/orchestrator_spec.md` | ~4000  | pipeline + idempotency skills | ~500   | 88%     |
````

**Required optimization rules:**

```markdown
1. **Reuse context within session** — Never re-read a document already loaded
2. **Skills first, docs second** — Load skills before falling back to raw documentation
3. **Prefer grouped execution** — Use Mode 2 or Mode 3 to share context
4. **No full-doc reads** — Load the relevant skill, then deep-dive into specific sections only
5. **Progressive loading** — skill discovery → skill body → doc section (only if needed)
6. **Skill injection is automatic** — `run_parallel.sh` injects skill references into every call
```

> `CORE_SKILLS` variable in `run_parallel.sh` lists all 28 skills injected automatically.

---

### 7. Resilience Framework

The most critical section. Documents how the system handles failures at every level.

**Required subsections:**

#### 7.1 Universal Retry Pattern

````markdown
```text
execute → validate → fix → re-validate → bounded retry → success OR rollback
```
````

Every code path terminates in a defined state — **no infinite loops, no undefined state**.

````

#### 7.2 Retry Configuration

Must match the variables in `run_parallel.sh` exactly:

```markdown
```bash
MAX_RETRIES_PHASE_BUILDER=5
MAX_RETRIES_DTO=5
MAX_RETRIES_INTEGRATION=5
MAX_RETRIES_MERGE=5
MAX_RETRIES_GLOBAL_VALIDATION=5
MAX_REMEDIATION_RETRIES=3
MAX_PARALLEL_AGENTS=3
MODEL_HEAVY=claude-opus-4.6
MODEL_HEAVY_LITE=claude-sonnet-4.6
````

````

#### 7.3 Workspace Confinement

```markdown
All agent prompts include a `_WORKSPACE_CONSTRAINT` clause injected by `run_parallel.sh`:

````

WORKSPACE CONSTRAINT: NEVER write any files, scripts, summaries, or reports to
/tmp, /var, /private, or any path outside this project directory. Write ALL output
files inside the project — use .parallel-dev/ for temporary artifacts.

```

```

#### 7.4 Checkpoint & Rollback

````markdown
Before each phase/group, a Git tag checkpoint is created:

```bash
git tag checkpoint-${phase_label}-pre
```
````

On retry limit exceeded:

```bash
git reset --hard checkpoint-${phase_label}-pre
```

````

#### 7.5 Agent Pipeline (per phase/group)

Must show the exact `AGENT_PIPELINE` from `run_parallel.sh`:

```markdown
```text
phase-builder (up to MAX_RETRIES_PHASE_BUILDER retries)
  → dto-guardian (up to MAX_RETRIES_DTO retries)
    → integration (up to MAX_RETRIES_INTEGRATION retries)
      → security-auditor
        → test-builder
          → refactor/quality gates (up to MAX_REMEDIATION_RETRIES retries)
            → success OR rollback
````

````

#### 7.6 Quality Gate Checks

Document the gates implemented in `scripts/hooks/quality-gates.sh`.
Language-specific commands go in the hook — NOT in `run_parallel.sh`.

**Required gates list:**

```markdown
| # | Check                  | Blocking? | Language-specific command (in hook)     |
|---|------------------------|-----------|-----------------------------------------|
| 1 | Compile/import check   | Yes       | `{compile-check}`                       |
| 2 | Lint check             | Yes       | `{lint-cmd}`                            |
| 3 | Test suite             | Yes       | `{test-cmd}`                            |
| 4 | No DB driver in modules| Yes       | grep for `{import-check}`               |
| 5 | No cross-module imports | Yes      | `python check_cross_module_imports.py`  |
| 6 | No unstructured output | Yes       | grep `console.log` / `print(` in `{module-dir}` |
| 7 | DTO immutability       | Yes       | grep for mutable defaults in `{contracts}` |
| 8 | Orchestrator integrity | Yes       | no DB adapter import in `{module-dir}` |
| 9 | Protected file changes | Advisory  | warn if `contracts/`, `database/`, `docs/` modified |
|10 | Deterministic ordering | Advisory  | no unordered iteration in `{module-dir}` |
````

Gates 1–8 are **blocking** (non-zero exit = failure). Gates 9–10 are **advisory**.

#### 7.7 Post-Phase Pipeline Stages

```markdown
| Stage               | Agent            | Function                                | Fatal?        |
| ------------------- | ---------------- | --------------------------------------- | ------------- |
| `post-merge-review` | `merge-reviewer` | DTO flow, module boundaries, orch auth  | Yes           |
| `docs-sync`         | `merge-reviewer` | Implementation drift from `docs/` specs | No (advisory) |
| `global-validation` | orchestrator     | Quality gates + orchestrator check      | Yes           |
| `remediation`       | `refactor`       | Fix global-validation failures          | Yes           |
```

#### 7.8 Per-Mode Recovery

```markdown
| Mode   | Failure Scope          | Recovery Action                                        |
| ------ | ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| Mode 1 | Single agent fails     | Rollback that phase. Other agents continue.            |
| Mode 1 | Merge conflict         | `conflict-resolver` agent (up to 5 retries).           |
| Mode 2 | Agent fails mid-group  | Rollback to checkpoint. Earlier commits preserved.     |
| Mode 2 | Context window full    | Split remaining phases into new session.               |
| Mode 3 | Single group fails     | Rollback that group. Other groups continue.            |
| All    | Post-merge review fail | `merge-reviewer` retries (up to 5). → `review_failed`. |
| All    | Global validation fail | `refactor` (up to 5). → `remediation_failed`.          |
```

---

### 8. Status Display

Document the output of `./scripts/run_parallel.sh status` so operators know what to expect.

**Required subsections:**

1. Full status layout (ASCII block showing all fields)
2. Phase/group state values table
3. State file location

```markdown
### Full Status Layout
```

═══ Parallel Development Status ═══

Mode: 3 (Hybrid)
Phases: <phase list>
Integration branch: integration/parallel-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS
Status: running
Started: <ISO timestamp>

Agent Status:
Phase/Group State Model Exit Updated
──────────────────── ────────── ──────────────────────── ────── ────────────────
phase-N (<name>) complete claude-opus-4.6 0 <timestamp>
phase-M (<name>) running claude-sonnet-4.5 — <timestamp>
──── Post-Phase Pipeline ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
post-merge-review complete claude-sonnet-4.6 0 <timestamp>
docs-sync advisory_failed claude-sonnet-4.5 1 <timestamp>
global-validation complete N/A 0 <timestamp>

```

### Phase/Group State Values

| State             | Meaning                                              |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| `running`         | Agent pipeline actively executing                    |
| `complete`        | All stages passed; exit code 0                       |
| `failed`          | Exceeded retry limit; rolled back to checkpoint      |
| `timed_out`       | Per-phase timeout expired (exit code 124)            |
| `advisory_failed` | Docs-sync advisory check reported issues (non-fatal) |

### State File Location

- Per-phase status: `.parallel-dev/phase-status.json`
- Session state: `.parallel-dev/state.json`
```

---

### 9. Requirements

List all tools the operator must have installed. Separate universal requirements
from language-specific ones. Language-specific tools belong in `scripts/hooks/` —
this section just documents what the hook needs.

**Required universal tools:**

```markdown
- **Bash 4+** — Required for associative arrays. macOS ships with bash 3.2; install via `brew install bash`
- **Git 2.5+** — Worktree support required for Mode 1 and Mode 3
- **Python 3** — Used by `run_parallel.sh` YAML parser (stdlib only, no packages)
- **Copilot CLI** — `npm install -g @github/copilot && copilot login`
- **GitHub CLI (`gh`)** — PR creation. Auto-installed if absent. Run `gh auth login` once
- **`COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN`, `GH_TOKEN`, or `GITHUB_TOKEN`** — Checked at startup
- **(Optional)** `timeout` / `gtimeout` — Per-phase timeout enforcement
```

**Language-specific section (project-fills):**

```markdown
**Language-specific tools** (implemented in `scripts/hooks/quality-gates.sh`):

| Tool                   | Install           | Used for              |
| ---------------------- | ----------------- | --------------------- |
| `{runtime-tool}`       | <install command> | Running the project   |
| `{package-manager}`    | <install command> | Dependency management |
| `{lint-cmd}` tool      | <install command> | Lint gate             |
| `{compile-check}` tool | <install command> | Compile gate          |
```

---

### 10. Fully Autonomous Pipeline

Show the complete end-to-end flow from `./scripts/run_parallel.sh start` to PR creation.
This is the "big picture" section.

**Required flow diagram:**

````markdown
```text
./scripts/run_parallel.sh start [--mode=1|2|3] <phases...>
        │
        ▼
[1] Per phase/group:
    phase-builder → dto-guardian → integration → security-auditor → test-builder → refactor
    (bounded retries per stage; rollback to checkpoint on exceed)
        │
        ▼
[2] Auto-merge all branches (union strategy)
    └─ conflict-resolver resolves conflicts (bounded retries)
        │
        ▼
[3] Post-merge review (merge-reviewer)
    DTO flow + module boundaries + orchestrator authority
        │
        ▼
[4] Docs sync (merge-reviewer, advisory)
        │
        ▼
[5] Global validation
    quality gates + orchestrator integrity
        │
        ▼
[6] PR created automatically via `gh pr create`
```
````

`````

> **phases.yaml alignment:** The `<phases...>` argument maps directly to phase numbers
> in `config/phases.yaml`. The `name`, `group`, and `complexity` fields determine how
> `run_parallel.sh` assigns models and organizes worktrees.

---

## Complete PARALLEL_DEV.md Template

Copy this block as the starting point for any new project's `docs/PARALLEL_DEV.md`.
Substitute `{…}` placeholders from the [Language Reference Table](#language-reference-table)
and the project's `config/phases.yaml`.

````markdown
# Parallel Development Guide — `run_parallel.sh`

> Operator guide for running multiple implementation phases simultaneously using
> autonomous AI agents. Supports 3 execution modes that balance speed, cost, and
> merge complexity.

---

## 1. Overview

This framework supports parallel development of pipeline phases. Most phases own isolated
modules under `{module-dir}` and communicate only through immutable DTOs in `{contracts}`.
This isolation enables parallel development — multiple phases implemented at the same time
by independent AI agents.

| Dimension      | More Parallelism              | Less Parallelism        |
| -------------- | ----------------------------- | ----------------------- |
| **Speed**      | Faster wall-clock time        | Slower (sequential)     |
| **Token cost** | Higher (each agent re-reads)  | Lower (shared context)  |
| **Merge risk** | More conflicts at integration | Fewer conflicts         |
| **Debugging**  | Harder (concurrent sessions)  | Easier (single session) |

---

## 2. Model Routing Strategy

| Mode                     | Heavy Model         | Used For                               |
| ------------------------ | ------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| Mode 1 (Full Parallel)   | `claude-opus-4.6`   | Heaviest phase (most complex by score) |
| Mode 2 (Token-Optimized) | `claude-sonnet-4.6` | Single session (all phases)            |
| Mode 3 (Hybrid)          | `claude-sonnet-4.6` | Heaviest group (by complexity score)   |

**Rotation pool:** `claude-sonnet-4.6 → claude-sonnet-4.5 → gpt-5.3-codex → gpt-5.4`

```bash
MODEL_HEAVY="claude-opus-4.6"
MODEL_HEAVY_LITE="claude-sonnet-4.6"
`````

---

## 3. Mode Definitions

### Mode 1 — Full Parallel (Maximum Speed)

<!-- See Section Definitions § Mode 1 for the full template -->

**When to use:** Deadline pressure; all phases independent.

### Mode 2 — Token-Optimized (Serial Grouping)

<!-- See Section Definitions § Mode 2 for the full template -->

**Grouping rules:** Max 3 phases; dependency order; DTO-producers before consumers.

**When to use:** Cost-sensitive; phases have sequential dependencies.

### Mode 3 — Hybrid (Balanced) — DEFAULT

<!-- See Section Definitions § Mode 3 for the full template -->

**When to use:** Default; phases have natural groupings.

---

## 4. Mode Selection Strategy

| Scenario                                    | Recommended Mode |
| ------------------------------------------- | ---------------- |
| Single phase implementation                 | Mode 2           |
| 2–3 phases with sequential dependency       | Mode 2           |
| 2–3 fully independent phases                | Mode 1           |
| 4+ phases, mix of dependent and independent | Mode 3           |
| Cost-constrained (limited premium requests) | Mode 2           |
| Deadline pressure, all phases independent   | Mode 1           |
| Default / unsure                            | Mode 3           |

---

## 5. Phase Grouping Rules

<!-- Fill in from config/phases.yaml group assignments -->

| Phase   | Owns                       | Group |
| ------- | -------------------------- | ----- |
| Phase 0 | `database/`, `{contracts}` | A     |
| Phase N | `{module-dir}/<name>/`     | B     |

Safe: phases in different groups ‖ phases with disjoint file ownership
Unsafe: Phase 0 ‖ anything; DTO changes ‖ consuming module changes

---

## 6. Token Cost Optimization Strategy

| Full Doc                    | Tokens | Equivalent Skill              | Savings |
| --------------------------- | ------ | ----------------------------- | ------- |
| `docs/architecture.md`      | ~5000  | pipeline + modularity skills  | 88%     |
| `docs/dto_contracts.md`     | ~6000  | dto skill                     | 93%     |
| `docs/orchestrator_spec.md` | ~4000  | pipeline + idempotency skills | 88%     |

Rules: reuse context; skills first; prefer grouped execution; no full-doc reads; progressive loading.

---

## 7. Resilience Framework

```bash
# All limits from run_parallel.sh defaults
MAX_RETRIES_PHASE_BUILDER=5
MAX_RETRIES_DTO=5
MAX_RETRIES_INTEGRATION=5
MAX_RETRIES_MERGE=5
MAX_RETRIES_GLOBAL_VALIDATION=5
MAX_REMEDIATION_RETRIES=3
MAX_PARALLEL_AGENTS=3
```

**Agent pipeline:** `phase-builder → dto-guardian → integration → security-auditor → test-builder → refactor`
**On failure:** rollback to `git tag checkpoint-${phase_label}-pre`

**Quality Gates** (implemented in `scripts/hooks/quality-gates.sh`):

| #   | Check                          | Blocking? |
| --- | ------------------------------ | --------- |
| 1   | `{compile-check}`              | Yes       |
| 2   | `{lint-cmd}`                   | Yes       |
| 3   | `{test-cmd}`                   | Yes       |
| 4   | No DB driver in `{module-dir}` | Yes       |
| 5   | No cross-module imports        | Yes       |
| 6   | No unstructured output         | Yes       |
| 7   | DTO immutability               | Yes       |
| 8   | Orchestrator integrity         | Yes       |
| 9   | Protected file changes         | Advisory  |
| 10  | Deterministic ordering         | Advisory  |

---

## 8. Status Display

```bash
./scripts/run_parallel.sh status
```

States: `running` | `complete` | `failed` | `timed_out` | `advisory_failed`
Files: `.parallel-dev/phase-status.json`, `.parallel-dev/state.json`

---

## 9. Requirements

- Bash 4+ (`brew install bash` on macOS)
- Git 2.5+ (worktree support)
- Python 3 (stdlib only — YAML parser in `run_parallel.sh`)
- Copilot CLI (`npm install -g @github/copilot && copilot login`)
- GitHub CLI (`gh auth login`)
- `COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN` / `GH_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` set

**Language-specific** (implement in `scripts/hooks/quality-gates.sh`):

- `{runtime-tool}`, `{package-manager}`, `{lint-cmd}` tool, `{compile-check}` tool

---

## 10. Fully Autonomous Pipeline

```text
./scripts/run_parallel.sh start [--mode=1|2|3] <phases...>
  → [per phase] phase-builder → dto-guardian → integration → security-auditor → test-builder → refactor
  → auto-merge (conflict-resolver on conflict)
  → post-merge-review (merge-reviewer)
  → docs-sync (advisory)
  → global-validation
  → gh pr create
```

````

---

## phases.yaml Alignment Checklist

Before writing or reviewing `docs/PARALLEL_DEV.md`, verify `config/phases.yaml`:

```yaml
phases:
  N:
    name: "<kebab-case, matches phase title>"
    complexity: <1-10>
    group: "<A-Z — same group = sequential, different group = parallel>"
    skills: "<skills this phase needs>"
```

- [ ] Each phase listed in Section 5 (File Ownership) has a matching entry in `config/phases.yaml`
- [ ] Group letters in File Ownership table match `group` values in `config/phases.yaml`
- [ ] Retry values in Section 7.2 match defaults in `run_parallel.sh`
- [ ] `AGENT_PIPELINE` order in Section 7.5 matches `run_parallel.sh` variable
- [ ] `PROTECTED_PATHS` in run_parallel.sh (`contracts/`, `database/`, `docs/`) are noted as unsafe combinations in Section 5
- [ ] Model names in Section 2 match `MODEL_HEAVY` and `MODEL_HEAVY_LITE` defaults in `run_parallel.sh`

---

## Checklist — Before Finalizing PARALLEL_DEV.md

- [ ] All 10 sections present, none omitted
- [ ] Language column chosen from Language Reference Table and applied consistently
- [ ] Model routing table matches `run_parallel.sh` `MODEL_HEAVY` / `MODEL_HEAVY_LITE` defaults
- [ ] Retry limits in Section 7.2 match `run_parallel.sh` `MAX_RETRIES_*` defaults
- [ ] `AGENT_PIPELINE` sequence matches `run_parallel.sh` variable exactly
- [ ] Quality gate table uses `{module-dir}`, `{test-cmd}`, `{lint-cmd}`, `{compile-check}` from Language Reference Table
- [ ] Language-specific tools documented as belonging in `scripts/hooks/quality-gates.sh`
- [ ] File ownership table in Section 5 maps to `config/phases.yaml` group assignments
- [ ] Mode Selection table includes all 7 scenarios
- [ ] Status state values table includes all 5 states
- [ ] Workspace confinement clause documented in Section 7.3
- [ ] Checkpoint/rollback pattern documented in Section 7.4
- [ ] No hardcoded model names — use env override pattern from Section 2
````
